header.title: Readability Checker — Test Content with 8 Readability Formulas
header.description: Free online readability checker to analyze your content with 8 industry-standard formulas: Flesch-Kincaid, Gunning Fog, SMOG Index, Coleman-Liau, ARI, and more. Get actionable tips to simplify language, improve clarity, and boost reader engagement. Ideal for SEO content writers, educators, and bloggers.
header.keywords: readability checker, readability score, flesch kincaid calculator, readability test, content readability, smog index, seo readability

input.placeholder: Paste your article, blog post, or any written content here to check readability…

flesch.title: Flesch Reading Ease
flesch.scoreDesc: Score 0–100, higher = easier. Most common readability formula.

gradeLevel.title: Consensus Grade Level
gradeLevel.scoreInfo: Grade level scores represent the US school grade a reader needs. Grade 8 = an 8th-grader can understand. Lower is generally more accessible.

suggestions.title: Suggestions
suggestions.avgLength: Average sentence length is {count} words — aim for 15–20 words per sentence.
suggestions.complexWords: {pct}% of words have 3+ syllables — try simpler alternatives.
suggestions.longSentences: {count} sentence{plural} over 25 words — consider splitting them.
suggestions.lowReadability: Overall readability is low — try shorter sentences and simpler words.
suggestions.noParagraphs: No paragraph breaks detected — break content into smaller chunks.
suggestions.highSyllables: Average syllables per word is high — use shorter words where possible.

allGood.message: Great readability! Your content looks clear and accessible.

gradeEstimates.title: Grade Level Estimates
gradeEstimates.legend: FK = Flesch-Kincaid · Fog = Gunning Fog · CL = Coleman-Liau · ARI = Automated Readability · LW = Linsear Write · D-C = Dale-Chall

sentenceAnalysis.title: Sentence Analysis
sentenceAnalysis.longSentenceHint: {count} long sentence{plural} (>25 words) highlighted in amber
sentenceAnalysis.allGood: All sentences are within recommended length

writingTips.title: Writing Tips
list: Use active voice instead of passive voice
Prefer short words over long synonyms
Use bullet points to break up dense paragraphs
Aim for a Flesch score above 60 for general audiences
Read your content aloud — awkward = edit it

howToUse.step4Desc: Use the sentence and word analysis to rewrite complex passages

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.sampleText: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This simple sentence contains every letter of the alphabet. Readability tools help writers create content that is easy to understand for a broad audience.
examples.result: Grade level: 3 · Very easy to read · Suitable for general audience

items.question: What is readability in SEO?
items.answer: Readability measures how easy your content is to understand. Search engines favor content that is accessible to a wider audience, making readability an important SEO factor.

items.question: What is a good Flesch Reading Ease score?
items.answer: A score of 60-70 is considered standard and understandable by 13-15 year olds. Scores above 70 are fairly easy to read, while scores below 30 are very difficult.

items.question: How can I improve readability?
items.answer: Use shorter sentences, simpler words, active voice, bullet points, and clear headings. Break long paragraphs into smaller chunks.

items.question: Does readability affect rankings?
items.answer: Yes, search engines like Google consider user engagement signals. Content that is easy to read keeps users on the page longer, reducing bounce rates and improving rankings.
